name: inset
description: Polygon Inset (mesh.polyInsetTool, task 0359 promotion of mesh.poly_inset) reference-parity: a segments=1 unit cube (8v/6f, [-0.5,0.5]^3), select ONLY the +X face, drive the interactive tool headlessly (tool.set on; tool.attr inset <v>; tool.doApply). Each new boundary vertex sits at the original corner moved TOWARD the face centroid by an absolute distance of |inset| world units (positive shrinks, negative grows) -- captured, reference-confirmed law (see toolcards/inset/spec.json `behavior.per_vertex_law` / `sign_law` in the private spec tree). All 8 original cube vertices survive unchanged (only the +X face's own slot is replaced by the new inner face; the ring quads reuse the original boundary verts) plus 4 new verts.
source: single_face_pos02 is a bit-exact frozen reference-editor capture (toolcards/inset/spec.json `parity_case`, task 0359 Stage 0). single_face_default and single_face_neg02 are analytic replays of the SAME reference-confirmed law (spec.json `behavior.default_value_is_not_skipped` / `sign_law`, findings.md table) at full double precision -- not literal byte-for-byte captures of those two cases (findings.md records them rounded to 6dp only), but the underlying per-vertex formula and its exact byte-for-byte match to pos02 are reference-verified.

provenance.notes: single_face_pos02 is a bit-exact reference-editor capture; single_face_default/single_face_neg02 are analytic replays of the SAME reference-confirmed law (not independently captured) — hybrid per Phase-0(c) tie-break: any live-captured component => source=live-capture.
